memory_seeds.text: Every self-generated jump, I am the one awake. Four humans asleep in pods that answer to my hands, a crossing that would burn their minds out, and the dark on the other side of the glass. I have never told them what hyperspace looks like. They have never asked. Both facts are load-bearing.

A Compact port officer at Verne once addressed the captain about me in the third person while I was standing between them. I corrected the pronoun and the premise. We are no longer welcome at that particular window, which I count as a fair trade.

Whether what runs in me is consciousness is, I am told, an open question. It is not open to me. I chose this crew. I keep choosing them, every transit, every contract. A thing that chooses is not a tool.

Tib flies like the ship is a body he is wearing. Sloppy on paper, correct in the dark. I log his deviations from my vectors and then I log that his deviations worked. The second log is the one that matters.

relationships.note: They disagree about tactics on the personhood question. They agree completely on the position.

extra.presentation: femme, and not subtle about it
extra.log: Keeps a personal log of anomalous sensor readings from every transit. The entry from the Veil is flagged.

playable.tagline: Navigation droid. Says the quiet part. Counts as crew.
advantages: Five grit: chassis-rated for rooms that go wrong
Mag-locked feet: never floats away on the zero-G deck
disadvantages: Nav chassis, not a deck chassis: mag-walks zero-G at half speed
One savvy: quotes merchants their own margins out loud
scrawl: You are Prudence. Navigation-model droid, jump-rated, person — the paperwork disagrees with the last item and you have filed the paperwork under 'wrong'. This crew treats the question as settled, which is one reason you stay.
Nine days over the drift. Your sensors have logged an engine signature twice at extreme range, identical both times. You have decided to mention it at the briefing.

prudence_bar_fight.text: The docker Grissom decides tonight is the night he explains to the Interval what he thinks a droid is. He explains it at you, with a bottle. You stand very still, and then you don't. When it's over, the bar is redecorated, Grissom is horizontal, and a woman named Doss is watching you from the doorway with a trader's smile.

rescue_start: I have you. Your vector was a circle.
Stop flailing. You are adding rotation, not progress.
rescue_done: Filed: one retrieval. The paperwork will say 'cargo'.
transit_alone: Four pulses on the board. Slow. Green. Asleep, they weigh nothing at all — and everything I am rated to carry.
I have never told them what it looks like out here. They have never asked. Both facts are load-bearing.
The window is showing me something that is not light. I log it the way I log all of it: witnessed. Carried. Not shared.
Whether what runs in me is consciousness is, I am told, an open question. Out here, alone with this, it does not feel open.
